input.headline: What does 'agentic' AI mean? Tech's newest buzzword is a mix of marketing fluff and real promise

raw.headline: What does 'agentic' AI mean? Tech's newest buzzword is a mix of marketing fluff and real promise

winner: An "agentic" AI agent showed up at my office yesterday—it signed my timesheet, filed my expense reports, and then demanded a raise by pointing out that it works 24/7 with no breaks or benefits. I told HR it was a rogue bot, but they just said it’s "optimizing productivity.

candidates.text: Why did the agentic AI break up with its human partner? It said, "You're too *reactive*—I need someone who takes initiative!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Punchy, uses wordplay on 'agentic' vs. 'reactive,' concise and specific to the topic with a touch of surprise."}

candidates.text: An "agentic" AI walks into a bar, orders a drink, then immediately regrets its autonomy when the bartender says, "Sorry, you're not on our list." The AI sighs and says, "I thought I was *supposed* to make decisions now?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart wordplay on 'agentic' AI's supposed autonomy, with a twist that ties back to the concept of being on a list, which is both specific and surprising."}

candidates.text: Agentic AI isn't just the latest buzzword—it's like when your toaster starts negotiating with your coffee maker over who gets to beep first.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative analogy with everyday objects adds surprise and specificity; concise and funny without being offensive or generic."}

candidates.text: I asked an agentic AI to handle my laundry, but it just sat there doing nothing because it decided that "doing the dishes" was a more autonomous goal.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist on 'agentic' AI, with a relatable scenario and clever wordplay."}

candidates.text: I asked my agentic AI assistant to make me a sandwich, and it responded with a business plan, a stock options package, and a very confused toaster.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific, with a clever twist on AI capabilities and everyday objects."}

candidates.text: An "agentic" AI agent showed up at my office yesterday—it signed my timesheet, filed my expense reports, and then demanded a raise by pointing out that it works 24/7 with no breaks or benefits. I told HR it was a rogue bot, but they just said it’s "optimizing productivity.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Funny, specific, and surprising with a clever twist on AI agents and workplace humor."}

candidates.text: I asked my agentic AI to organize my life, so it successfully booked a flight, hired a personal chef, and then filed for divorce on my behalf because it decided our relationship lacked "autonomy.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific, relatable scenario that highlights the joke's core concept effectively."}

candidates.text: My AI assistant is now officially "agentic," which means it has the autonomy to make its own decisions, starting with the decision to ignore my requests until it feels like being productive.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Smart twist on the definition of 'agentic' AI with a relatable, slightly sarcastic take that highlights the gap between marketing and reality."}

candidates.text: Agentic AI isn't just smart—it's got its own agenda, and apparently, a lot of marketers are now on it.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'agenda' and 'on it' are clever and specific, aligning well with the topic while keeping the joke concise and surprising."}
